text.tspan: SANCHITHA SUDARSHANA
IST 719
How did Airbnb prices change across NYC boroughs during COVID?
How did the distribution of nightly prices shift across different room types
from pre-pandemic to post-pandemic periods?
Did the Airbnb market structurally shift toward higher
price tiers as it transitioned through COVID eras ?

path.aria-label: Airbnb Price Shifts By Room Type (Pre vs Post COVID)
Airbnb Price Tier Composition Across COVID Eras
COVID−era Changes In Airbnb Prices Across NYC Boroughs

text.tspan: Data sourced - Kaggle
(https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/arianazmoudeh/airbnbopendata)
Analysis and visualization - R
Packages - dplyr, ggplot2, ggridges, ggalluvial, ggpubr, scales
Poster design - Inkscape
Story
This poster quantifies how Airbnb prices in New York City shifted
across COVID eras by room type, neighborhood, price tier, and
distribution shape. Using normalized price indices, distribution shifts,
and listing flows, it reveals uneven recovery patterns that reshaped the
city’s short-term rental market .
Motivation
Travelers and hosts encountered a more unpredictable market as
Airbnb pricing in New York City changed during and afterCOVID-19.
While hosts, city planners, and researchers require clarity on how pricing
patterns varied among room types, areas, and price tiers, travelers want to
know where costs changed.
Data Description
This analysis uses the New York City Airbnb Open Dataset,
originally containing 102,599 listings and 26 variables.
The data was subset to 25,520 listings from 2018–2022 and
cleaned by removing missing construction years, restricting listings
to NYC boroughs, standardizing price fields, and excluding
non-positive prices. Listings were grouped into
pre-pandemic, pandemic, and post-pandemic eras to
support comparative price analysis.
AirBnb Pricing Dynamics in New York City Across COVID Eras

path.aria-label: Distribution of Airbnb Nightly Prices in NYC
